FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class DirectWriteFontSystem

Namespace
FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite
Assembly
FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite.dll

The DirectWrite text backend (IFontSystem) — a thin wrapper over TextLayoutEngine. Every member (measurement AND the editor queries) runs the full itemize → shape → wrap/trim → position pipeline (kerning, ligatures, complex script, UAX #14 line breaking, CJK/emoji font fallback), so the measured box, the hit-tested caret and the selection rects all come from the SAME layout the renderer — which uses the same engine — actually draws. measure ≡ hit-test ≡ render: this parity is what keeps shaped glyphs from overlapping (the divergence that read as bold when the renderer was shaped but measurement was per-char). Touched on the UI thread during phase-6 layout and at edit/drag time; the render thread owns a separate engine instance, so queries here can never corrupt render-side buffers. Query results live in the engine's reused grow-only tables — 0 steady-state allocation per keystroke/pointer-move.

public sealed class DirectWriteFontSystem : IFontSystem, IDisposable
Inheritance
object
DirectWriteFontSystem

IFontSystem

Methods

Dispose()

Performs application-defined tasks associated with freeing, releasing, or resetting unmanaged resources.

public void Dispose()

GetCaret(ReadOnlySpan<char>, in TextStyle, float, int, out float, out float, out float, out int)

Caret geometry for a UTF-16 index: x + the line's top/height (single line: top = 0, height = the line height) + line index. Returns the LEADING position of charIndex; at a soft-wrap boundary the leading position is the START of the continuation line — trailing affinity (pinning the caret to the END of the wrapped line) is the CALLER's job. charIndex == text length → the trailing edge of the last line. An index inside a multi-char cluster snaps to the cluster's edges. Editor-hosting backends must implement this; the default throws.

public void GetCaret(ReadOnlySpan<char> text, in TextStyle style, float maxWidth, int charIndex, out float x, out float lineTop, out float lineHeight, out int lineIndex)

Parameters

text ReadOnlySpan<char>
style TextStyle
maxWidth float
charIndex int
x float
lineTop float
lineHeight float
lineIndex int

GetRangeRects(ReadOnlySpan<char>, in TextStyle, float, int, int, Span<RectF>)

One rect per line fragment covered by [start, end), x-from/x-to via cluster edges. Returns the count written (≤ rects.Length; excess fragments are dropped — callers size generously). Zero-width ranges produce nothing. Editor-hosting backends must implement this; the default throws.

public int GetRangeRects(ReadOnlySpan<char> text, in TextStyle style, float maxWidth, int start, int end, Span<RectF> rects)

Parameters

text ReadOnlySpan<char>
style TextStyle
maxWidth float
start int
end int
rects Span<RectF>

Returns

int

HitTestText(ReadOnlySpan<char>, in TextStyle, float, Point2, out bool)

Point → UTF-16 index. Returns the SNAPPED insertion index — the leading or trailing edge of the hit glyph cluster — and trailing reports which half of the cluster was hit. Points outside the laid-out bounds clamp to the nearest valid index (above → first line, below → last line, left → line start, right → line end), so drag-selection keeps tracking past the box. Editor-hosting backends must implement this; the default throws (the experimental GDI path cannot host editable text).

public int HitTestText(ReadOnlySpan<char> text, in TextStyle style, float maxWidth, Point2 point, out bool trailing)

Parameters

text ReadOnlySpan<char>
style TextStyle
maxWidth float
point Point2
trailing bool

Returns

int

Measure(StringId, in TextStyle, float)

Measure a string under a style (intrinsic content size). Feeds the layout engine. When maxWidth is finite and the style wraps, the result is the word-wrapped multi-line box.

public TextMetrics Measure(StringId text, in TextStyle style, float maxWidth = Infinity)

Parameters

text StringId
style TextStyle
maxWidth float

Returns

TextMetrics
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
